title: Wi‑Fi help visit
description: Router setup, weak signal issues, dead zones and slow connections.
included: Check the router, cables, placement and basic settings
Help with poor signal in one room or across the house
Advice on boosting speed, coverage or Wi‑Fi stability
note: Typical visit: around 45–60 minutes. If extra equipment is needed, I’ll tell you before buying anything.

title: Laptop or PC health check
description: Slow computers, updates, backups and everyday troubleshooting.
included: Speed up a sluggish laptop or desktop
Check for updates, storage issues and missing software
Help with backup, file access and general reliability
note: Good for the “it’s just getting slower” sort of problem. I’ll explain what’s worth fixing and what’s not.

title: Email help and account recovery
description: Outlook, Gmail, forwarding, password issues and mailbox setup.
included: Set up email on a phone, tablet or new laptop
Help with password reset, recovery or forwarding rules
Sort out spam, inbox clutter or missing messages
note: Straightforward support for the sort of email problems that seem small until they become annoying.

title: Printer, phone and device setup
description: Getting new kit working properly without the usual headache.
included: Set up printers, phones, tablets or home devices
Connect things to Wi‑Fi and fix basic connection problems
Make sure the kit actually works the way it should
note: Perfect for new purchases, replacements or “why won’t this just connect?” moments.

title: Home office setup
description: Getting your computer, printer, router and phone working together.
included: Set up a new desk setup or tidy up a messy one
Connect devices, printer, router and email in one go
Leave you with something that is actually usable
note: Best when you want a few things sorted in one visit instead of piecemeal fixes.
